impact_cards.label: Goal-End
impact_cards.description: Standard power-play goals only, labeled by the game state immediately before the shot/goal event (5v4, 4v3, or 5v3).

impact_cards.label: Box Exit + 5s
impact_cards.description: Counts goals during continuous play until the penalized player exits the box, then adds up to 5 extra seconds unless a stoppage or goal ends the edge first.

impact_cards.label: First PK Possession
impact_cards.description: Counts goals during continuous play until the former penalty-kill team records the first clear possession event after expiry, with any stoppage or goal ending the carryover sooner.

impact_cards.label: First PK Change
impact_cards.description: Counts goals during continuous play until both former PK defencemen who were still on the ice at expiry have changed, or until an earlier non-icing stoppage or goal ends the edge first.

rows.penalty_type: Too Many Men On The Ice

officials.note: Public NHL data does not assign each penalty to a specific referee, so this section shows the penalty environment in the games each referee worked. The clearest signal is non-offsetting minors per game: minor or bench-minor penalties that were not matched by an equal-duration minor from the other team at the same stoppage.

notes: Current-season PP minutes come from shifts tagged PP in the public NHL shift feed, while PP and 5v5 points come from goal events plus official assists.
The 5v5 comparison traits reuse the same public rankings metrics shown elsewhere on the site, so the PP page speaks the same language as the rankings page.

Career curves now use sequential 60-minute PP buckets instead of a separate early-career sample. When a single game crosses a bucket boundary, its PP points are apportioned by PP minutes so every bucket stays usage-based.
Draft-to-present coverage is still missing for some qualified-skater career seasons (2004-2005); the charts use the loaded subset for now.
Career coverage in this database currently spans 2005-2006 through 2025-2026.
